skill.name: context-fundamentals
skill.description: Understand context anatomy, context limits, and progressive disclosure patterns for agent systems.
skill.summary: Learn the core concepts needed to design efficient agent context.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and a network indicator. AI review found these are false positives from Markdown fences, educational prose, prompt examples, and a source URL in metadata. No executable scripts, data exfiltration behavior, or prompt injection attempt was found in the reviewed SKILL.md content.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fence Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings occur in fenced Markdown examples. They do not execute commands, call an interpreter, or pass user input to a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is limited to Markdown code fences and static educational examples. No executable file, script block, shell invocation, or command interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: Source URL Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URL is a source metadata link in the skill header. It is not used by code and does not initiate an outbound network request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only in frontmatter metadata. The skill contains no HTTP client code, fetch call, webhook, or instruction to transmit data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography matches appear in prose about context, Markdown structure, tool definitions, and context budgets. No cryptographic API, hashing function, cipher, or security-sensitive comparison is implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is prose-only documentation. The flagged lines do not contain cryptographic primitives, credential handling, or runnable code.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Conceptual Prose
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reconnaissance matches describe context-management strategies and context degradation. They do not collect host details, enumerate files, or run system inspection commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are natural-language guidance about agent design. No command names, filesystem enumeration logic, or environment inspection code appears in the evidence.

content.value_statement: Agent teams often struggle to decide what belongs in context and what should load later. This skill explains context anatomy, attention limits, and progressive disclosure patterns for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains the major parts of agent context, including prompts, tools, retrieved documents, history, and tool outputs.
Describes why context windows are finite and why longer context can reduce precision.
Introduces progressive disclosure as a method for loading information only when needed.
Gives practical guidance for context budgeting, compaction thresholds, and attention-aware placement.
Shows simple Markdown examples for organizing system prompts and staged document loading.
limitations: It is educational documentation and does not implement automated context measurement.
It does not provide model-specific token limits or pricing data.
It references related skills that may not be present in every installation.
It does not replace security review for prompts, tools, or retrieved content.

use_cases.title: Design an Agent Context Plan
use_cases.description: Use the concepts to decide which instructions, tools, documents, and history should be available at each stage.

use_cases.title: Debug Context-Related Failures
use_cases.description: Use the anatomy of context to identify whether poor results come from missing information, stale history, or overloaded context.

use_cases.title: Teach Context Engineering Basics
use_cases.description: Use the structured explanations to onboard teammates before deeper work on optimization, degradation, or tool design.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Context Parts
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an agent setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-fundamentals to list the context components in my agent design. Explain what each component contributes and what may be missing.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Progressive Disclosure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-fundamentals to redesign this agent context with progressive disclosure. Separate startup information from information that can load on demand.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Context Budget
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-fundamentals to propose a context budget for this workflow. Include what to keep, what to compact, and when to load references.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Agent Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-fundamentals to critique this agent architecture. Focus on attention placement, tool definition clarity, message history growth, and retrieval strategy.

output_examples.input: Review my agent prompt and tool list for context quality.
output: The system prompt belongs at startup because it defines persistent behavior.
The full API documentation should load only when an API task requires it.
The tool descriptions need clearer usage boundaries so the agent does not guess.

output_examples.input: Help me reduce context usage in a support automation agent.
output: Keep escalation rules and response tone in startup context.
Load product manuals by topic instead of placing every manual in the initial prompt.
Add a compaction trigger when conversation history begins to dominate the context.

output_examples.input: Explain why my long-context agent loses important details.
output_examples.output: The skill would explain attention budget limits, weaker retrieval from overloaded context, and the need to place critical details near attention-favored positions.

best_practices: Start with the smallest context that lets the agent make the next good decision.
Use clear section boundaries for instructions, background, tools, and expected outputs.
Review context growth during development and add compaction before quality degrades.
anti_patterns: Do not load every document at startup when most tasks need only a small subset.
Do not rely on vague high-level instructions when concrete behavior is required.
Do not assume a larger context window removes the need for context design.

faq.question: What does this skill teach?
faq.answer: It teaches how agent context is structured and why context quality matters for reliable model behavior.

faq.question: Does it run commands or change files?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only and provides conceptual guidance and examples.

faq.question: Is this useful for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The concepts apply to Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and other agent systems that manage prompts, tools, and history.

faq.question: What is progressive disclosure?
faq.answer: Progressive disclosure means loading only the information needed now, then loading more specific context when the task requires it.

faq.question: Does the skill provide token counting tools?
faq.answer: No. It explains context budgeting concepts but does not include an automated token counter.

faq.question: When should I use this skill first?
faq.answer: Use it before designing agent workflows, reviewing tool definitions, or debugging failures that may come from poor context.
